content.problemStatement:
Given a binary string SSS of length NNN and an integer KKK.

An operation on the string is defined as:


Select two indices iii and jjj (i≠j)(i \neq j)(i=j) such that  i %Ki \ \% Ki %K  is equal to j %Kj \ \% Kj %K;
Set both SiS_iSi​ and SjS_jSj​ as Si⊕SjS_i\oplus S_jSi​⊕Sj​, where ⊕\oplus⊕ denotes the bitwise XOR operation.
Determine the **minimum** number of operations required to obtain a string such that either all characters of the string are 000 or all characters of the string are 111.

Under the given constraints, it can be proven that the answer always exists.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two integer NNN the length of the string and an integer KKK separated by space.
The second line contains a binary string SSS.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the **minimum** number of operations required to obtain a string such that either all character of the string are 000 or all characters of the string are 111.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Since the string already contains all zeros, we do not need to apply any operation.
Test case 222: The given string is S=10S = 10S=10.

Select i=1i = 1i=1 and j=2j = 2j=2 (since i %K=j %K=0i \ \% K = j \ \% K = 0i %K=j %K=0) and set S1S_1S1​ and S2S_2S2​ as S1⊕S2S_1\oplus S_2S1​⊕S2​ = 111.

The string formed will be 111111 where all characters are 111. The minimum number of operations required is 111.
Test case 333: The given string is S=1100S = 1100S=1100.

Select i=1i = 1i=1 and j=3j = 3j=3 and set S1S_1S1​ and S3S_3S3​ as S1⊕S3S_1\oplus S_3S1​⊕S3​ = 111. The string formed will be 111011101110.
Select i=2i = 2i=2 and j=4j = 4j=4 and set S2S_2S2​ and S4S_4S4​ as S2⊕S4S_2\oplus S_4S2​⊕S4​ = 111. The string formed will be 111111111111 where all characters are 111.

The minimum number of operations required is 222.
